schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

identity.canonical_title: How These Map to the Math (Short Formulas): PSF(ρ,φ)

theory.canonical_statement: ## 2) How these map to the math (short formulas) - Aperture → PSF (Airy/Bessel): \[ \text{PSF}(\rho,\varphi)=\Big|\mathcal{F}\{A(\rho,\varphi)\}\Big|^2 \quad\text{with}\quad A(\rho,\varphi)=A_{\rm circ}(\rho)\Big(1+\sum_k \epsilon_k(\rho)\cos(m_k\varphi+\phi_k)\Big). \] Peaks at \(m_k\) produce \(m_k\)-fold spikes. - Spherical-harmonic content (TZP/vortex): \[ \Psi(\theta,\phi)\approx\sum_{\ell,m}\Psi_{\ell m}Y_{\ell}^{m}(\theta,\phi). \] A dominant band of \(m\)-values produces \(m\)-spoke angular structure; sign change across equator (Coriolis/dipole) ↔ opposite sign of \(\Psi_{\ell m}\) for northern vs southern hemispheres. - Separatrix / phase zero: \[ \text{Separatrix: } \Psi(\theta,\phi)=0,\quad\text{Phase singularity: } \Re\{E\}=\Im\{E\}=0 \] Both appear as lines in images and can be detected as zero-crossings of scalar fields (streamfunction or complex analytic field).
theory.technical_interpretation: How These Map to the Math (Short Formulas): PSF(ρ,φ) is a fourier/spectral transform, written PSF(ρ,φ)= |FA(ρ,φ) |^2 with A(ρ,φ)=A_rm circ(ρ) (1+∑_k ε_k(ρ)cos(m_kφ+φ_k) ).. It maps the quantity into the frequency or spectral domain, exposing its harmonic content. It is built from ρ, φ, F, A, A_ circ, k, defining PSF(ρ,φ). Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.inputs: ρ, φ, F, A, A_ circ, k
